claim: Fine-tuning is worth considering when API costs exceed customer-pay revenue, evals plateau, or latency requirements cannot be met by frontier endpoints
actionable: Monitor these three signals: API costs > revenue, evals plateau, and latency SLOs; if any true, investigate fine-tuning ROI

claim: Supervised fine-tuning requires only 300 lines of Python; most AI engineers already have the infrastructure (agent harness, eval data) to start
actionable: If you have built an agent harness and collect eval data, you have what you need to fine-tune; start with supervised fine-tuning

claim: Reinforcement learning fine-tuning is massively embarrassingly parallel (rollout phase); serverless platforms like Modal can scale to 50,000+ sandboxes
actionable: Use serverless compute for RL rollout; fan out evaluations across thousands of containers on-demand

claim: Frontier labs optimize for universal performance; your fine-tuned model can win on your specific business logic and cost 1/5 to 1/10 of frontier APIs
actionable: Fine-tune toward your domain, not universal tasks; expect 5-10x cost reduction and better accuracy on your use cases

claim: Serving fine-tuned models requires vLLM, SG Lang, or Triton; can auto-scale to traffic on serverless platforms
actionable: Deploy fine-tuned models using vLLM on serverless platforms; auto-scale serving to match traffic patterns

claim: Start fine-tuning within 6-12 months of product launch if targeting differentiation; don't wait years to explore custom models
actionable: Plan fine-tuning early in product roadmap; begin collecting eval data and training data from day one
